text: Databases are essential tools for storing and organizing data. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases, like PostgreSQL, store data in structured tables with rows and columns. They use queries written in SQL to retrieve information. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible and store data in formats like documents or graphs. A key step in using any database is data modeling, which involves planning how data will be structured and related. To speed up searches, databases use indexes—special data structures that act like a book's index. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database for your application.
